title: The worst reviews of NGE EOE
display_title: The worst reviews of NGE EOE
wikitext:
[]: The worst reviews of NGE/EOE             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Sun Mar 07, 2010 10:58 pm Probably one of the worse ones is this little gem review on EOE http://www.themanime.org/viewreview.php?id=141 No 1: The reviewer never explained why he hated the philosophy in EOE No 2: He disliked it simply because it used non animated scenes. That's just a silly reason to dislike something. You have to review it as a whole, not on its medium. No 3:He seemed to hate it because it disturbed him psychologically. Join the crowd man. That doesn't change its quality. No 4:The mind rape has always been part of NGE, from the very beginning.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Mar 08, 2010 12:16 am If a work has struck you far enough to disturb you, then it has accomplished its intention.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Mar 08, 2010 12:44 am he probably was a weak willed daisy that just couldn't handle it, thus turned into mush by watching EoE

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Mon Mar 08, 2010 4:15 am it's weird considering that site has a lot of good reviews.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Mar 08, 2010 7:24 am Meh, I always avoid that sites reviews when looking for new shows to watch. Usually I find the explanations for the score of the series pretty vague. I guess this review just gives me more reason to disregard their opinions..

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon Mar 08, 2010 9:33 am It's not even worth it to review EoE. It's too subjective a work, and it just has way too much material to go into detail about it. I've made an attempt, once, but I just deleted the two pages on it that I had written so far and replaced it with "You have to see it for yourself."

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Mar 08, 2010 1:03 pm THEM anime reviews vary a LOT in quality. Sometimes their reviews can be biased simply because the reviewer didn't enjoy said anime or it disturbed them(which is the case with this guy's EOE review). Most people tend to dislike Eva on a subjective platform. It simply repulsed them so much that they can't stand it. From a purely objective platform, one cannot deny the fact that NGE and EOE are very good.

[]:             Quote Post by Apjak » Mon Mar 08, 2010 2:41 pm I don't like that he says Anno made an error with the second half of the movie. This is pure semantics, but error should mean that he tried to do something and failed, not that he succeeded in doing something that you didn't personally like. I have always felt Eva was made with a specific audience in mind, wrapped up in a package that appears to be for a much broader audience. Then everybody grew up, and Eva became the mainstream pap.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Mon Mar 08, 2010 2:48 pm Grimmjow wrote: From a purely objective platform, one cannot deny the fact that NGE and EOE are very good.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Mar 08, 2010 4:08 pm I need to write a review of Evangelion. Now that would be the worst Evangelion review of all time.

[]:             Quote Post by kirri » Mon Mar 08, 2010 4:31 pm For some reason the website won't load for me anymore, but I remember reading this review because it was linked in the EoE wikipedia entry, and thinking how terrible it was. The guy clearly states he has no intention of looking deeper in to the meaning of anything, and then he goes on to insult Anno and calls EoE "nihilistic" which is just false. I also think it's funny how he says "it kinda made me think about life...I guess" Um, that's the whole point

[]:             Quote Post by Kaworu Mark.06 » Mon Mar 08, 2010 4:58 pm When it comes to reviewing stuff, you can't let your personal feelings get in the way. You shouldn't even have someone who hates it review it. That's just gonna push people away from stuff that they might have become fans of.

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Mon Mar 08, 2010 5:20 pm Kaworu Mark.06 wrote: When it comes to reviewing stuff, you can't let your personal feelings get in the way. You shouldn't even have someone who hates it review it. That's just gonna push people away from stuff that they might have become fans of.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaworu Mark.06 » Mon Mar 08, 2010 5:43 pm Oz wrote: Kaworu Mark.06 wrote: When it comes to reviewing stuff, you can't let your personal feelings get in the way. You shouldn't even have someone who hates it review it. That's just gonna push people away from stuff that they might have become fans of.

[]:             Quote Post by BGMLV » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:41 pm T.H.E.M Anime Reviews is the worst site I have ever witnessed in the history of internet. It needs to be cut down. T_T

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Mon Mar 08, 2010 6:44 pm BGMLV wrote: T.H.E.M Anime Reviews is the worst site I have ever witnessed in the history of internet. It needs to be cut down. T_T

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 08, 2010 8:39 pm I've read plenty of bad reviews on NGE from a variety of sources. The reviewers have a tendency to be, well, let's say not all that sensitive to artistic greatness. I've replied to many on Amazon if anyone wants to look them up (I don't know why you would). The debate over subjectivity VS objectivity in art criticism is a complicated subject. The easy answer is that "it's relative", meaning that it's a relationship between how the subject interacts with the object and how the latter effects the former. The best critics aren't those that are the most objective per say, but the ones who are best at verbally processing and expressing their subjective experience of a work. Really, criticism is (at least) a three part process: observation, processing, and judgment. In essence, this is how we react to just about everything in life; most of it unconsciously and intuitive. But criticism is almost never arbitrary; even at its most ignorant. The best criticism almost always combines knowledge culled from experience, logic, descriptive ability, and how a work succeeds or fails on any given standard or its intentions. One has to tune those three levels. Criticism is like anything else in that it requires practice and dedication to be really good at.

[]:             Quote Post by flapjack » Mon Mar 08, 2010 11:36 pm bump

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Tue Mar 09, 2010 12:55 am It's near impossible to look at things with total objectivity, and I'm not surprised that this reviewer made it personal apparently because EoE was too complicated for his brain to handle. What if he watched, say, Brazil? I relied more on word-of-mouth whenever I look for something I want to watch. Edit: sometimes IMDB's user reviews tend to work in my favor. I take Ebert's commentaries with a grain of salt.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 09, 2010 1:46 am soul.assassin wrote: What if he watched, say, Brazil?
